resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def mergeAlternately(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> str:
        result = ""
        i, j = 0, 0
        while i < len(word1) or j < len(word2):
            if i < len(word1):
                result += word1[i]
                i += 1
            if j < len(word2):
                result += word2[j]
                j += 1
        return result
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: This solution uses two pointers, i and j, to iterate through word1 and word2 respectively. It alternates between adding characters from word1 and word2 to the result string. If one string is longer than the other, it simply appends the remaining characters from the longer string to the result. This approach has a high efficiency as it only iterates through the strings once and has a linear time complexity.
